title: Internet Brainrot
description: Psychological damage caused by excessive internet exposure.

stories.title: Office Emergency
stories.template: As Head of {jobTitle1}, I regret to announce that the {adjective1} {pluralNoun1} have learned how to {verb1}.

stories.title: Family Group Chat
stories.template: My {relative1} accidentally sent a photo of {food1} to the family group chat and now everyone thinks {celebrity1} is feeling {emotion1}.

stories.title: Terrible Superhero
stories.template: By day I am normal. By night I become The {adjective1} {food1}, protector of all innocent {pluralNoun1} who simply cannot {verb1}.

stories.title: Influencer Apology
stories.template: Hey guys, {name1} here. I want to apologize for the {object1}. I never meant to {verbPast1} anyone, and I am taking time to feel {emotion1}.

stories.title: AI Takeover
stories.template: The AI became self-aware after a {occupation1} spent 14 straight hours {verbIng1} at a {object1}, leaving humanity deeply {emotion1}.

stories.title: The Tweet
stories.template: At 3:17 AM I tweeted about {{subject}}. The take was simply: {{opinion}}. I woke up to {{notifs}} notifications, a quote-tweet from someone verified, and the slow realization that I would have to {{action}}.

stories.title: TikTok Comment Section
stories.template: The video was just {{video}}. The top comment said: {{topComment}}. It had 84,000 likes. Then {{username}} replied with {{reply}}, and now there are seventeen reaction videos and a podcast episode about it.

stories.title: YouTube Rabbit Hole
stories.template: It started innocently — I searched {{start}}. Two hours later, the autoplay had taken me through {{video1}}, then {{video2}}, then somehow {{video3}}. It is now 4 AM and I have an unshakeable belief that {{belief}}.

stories.title: AI Chatbot Confession
stories.template: I have been talking to {{botName}} for six weeks. Last night I told it {{confession}}, which I have not told a single human being. It responded with the most thoughtful advice: {{advice}}. I closed my laptop and immediately {{outcome}}.

stories.title: Discord Server Drama
stories.template: The {{serverName}} server had {{memberCount}} members and a delicate ecosystem. Then someone posted {{trigger}} in #{{channel}}. Within ninety minutes, three mods had resigned, the owner went private, and the head mod was demodded for {{ban}}. The server is now read-only. Nobody has spoken in twelve days.

stories.title: Podcast Bro Monologue
stories.template: Episode 412 opens with three minutes of breathing and then he says: today we are going to talk about {{topic}}. Halfway through, his guest — a {{guest}} — nods seriously as he claims {{claim1}}, citing the famous {{study}}. He brings up {{supplement}} four times. He tells you to question {{questionThing}}. The episode ends with a 90-second ad read for {{merch}}. Total runtime: 3 hours 47 minutes.

stories.title: Fancam Edit
stories.template: Someone made a fancam of {{subject}} set to {{song}} with {{effect}} every time they blink. The caption was: {{caption}}. It has {{views}} views. The comment section is currently at war over {{commentWar}}. {{stitch}} stitched it with a 14-minute response and now even more people are watching the original.

stories.title: Subreddit Moderator Meltdown
stories.template: r/{{subName}} had {{subscribers}} subscribers and exactly one full-time mod. Rule 7 was, very clearly: {{rule}}. Then someone posted {{post}}. The mod banned the user for {{banReason}}, then went into the wiki and {{modAction}}. Within a day, the sub became {{aftermath}}. The entire community fled to r/{{newName}}, where things are not better, just newer.

stories.title: Wikipedia Deep Dive
stories.template: I opened Wikipedia to fact-check one thing about {{start}}. From there I clicked through to {{page1}}, then {{page2}}, then for reasons I cannot reconstruct, {{page3}}. I learned that {{fact}}, which I cannot unlearn. I am now reading every article tagged "{{category}}." When I look up at the clock, it is {{fate}}.

stories.title: Stan Twitter War
stories.template: The {{fandom1}} and the {{fandom2}} had been peaceful for almost six hours. Then someone posted {{trigger}}. Within minutes, {{quoteTweet}} got 200,000 likes. By morning, {{trend}} was trending worldwide. The celebrity in question subtweeted: {{celebReaction}}. {{casualty}} deleted their account. Then everyone moved on because {{newDrama}} happened by lunchtime.

stories.title: Viral Cancellation Arc
stories.template: Day 1: {{person}} posted something on {{platform}}. Specifically: {{original}}. Day 2: Someone resurfaced {{screenshot}} from years ago. Day 3: {{defender}} defended them, which somehow made it worse. Day 4: The apology video dropped. It included {{apologyVideo}}, and a single shot of {{applesauce}} in the background that the internet would not stop talking about. Day 5: {{thinkpiece}} ran a thinkpiece. Day 6: {{spinoff}} happened, almost completely unrelated, dragging three more people in. Day 12: The comeback attempt was {{comeback}}. Six months later they are {{newCareer}}, and nobody is paying attention because everyone is busy with {{newDiscourse}}.

stories.title: Parasocial Spiral
stories.template: I have been watching the same {{creator}} make {{content}} for {{hours}} straight hours this month. I know {{fact1}}. I also know {{fact2}}, which is a thing only their close friends should know. Last night I had a dream where {{dream}}, and I have spent today imagining running into them {{encounter}}. The spiral truly started when {{moment}}, and at 2 AM I made the decision to {{decision}}. By morning I had already moved on to {{newCreator}}. I am telling myself the lesson is {{lesson}}, but we both know that is not what is happening.
